name: pandas
summary: Flexible data analysis and manipulation library for Python.

sections.name: I/O

functions.purpose: Read CSV file into a DataFrame
Read a Parquet file into a DataFrame
Write DataFrame to a CSV file
Write DataFrame to a Parquet file
Read a JSON file into a DataFrame

gotchas: Modifying a slice of a DataFrame can trigger SettingWithCopyWarning.
pd.read_csv() infers dtypes from the first chunk of rows; columns with mixed types later in the file may silently coerce to object dtype, inflating memory and breaking downstream numeric operations.

sections.name: Inspection

functions.purpose: Return the first n rows of the DataFrame
Print a concise summary of the DataFrame including dtypes and memory usage
Generate descriptive statistics for numeric columns
Return the data types of each column as a Series
Return a Series containing counts of unique rows in the DataFrame

gotchas: df.describe() only includes numeric columns by default; categorical and datetime columns are silently excluded unless you pass include='all', which can mislead EDA by hiding missing data in non-numeric columns.
df.dtypes returns a pandas Series, not a dictionary; comparing it with a plain dict for equality will always return False even when the mappings are identical.

sections.name: Selection

functions.purpose: Access a group of rows and columns by labels or boolean array
Access a group of rows and columns by integer position
Select one or more columns by name
Filter rows using a query expression string
Select rows where the boolean mask is True

gotchas: df.loc[] with integer labels on a non-RangeIndex DataFrame selects by label, not position; using df.loc[0] on a reindexed DataFrame may select a row with index label 0 even if it is not the first row.

Chained boolean indexing like df[df['a'] > 0][df['b'] < 0] evaluates the second filter on the original DataFrame, not the filtered one, because each bracket creates a new DataFrame with a potentially reset index.

sections.name: Cleaning

functions.purpose: Remove missing values from the DataFrame
Fill NA/NaN values with a specified value or method
Remove duplicate rows based on specified columns
Cast a pandas object to a specified dtype
Alter axes labels

gotchas: df.fillna(0) on an object-dtype column silently converts the column to object strings '0' instead of numeric 0, causing subsequent numeric operations to raise TypeError.
df.drop_duplicates() defaults to considering all columns; forgetting to specify subset can silently remove valid rows that differ in an irrelevant column like a timestamp or row number.

sections.name: Aggregation

functions.purpose: Group DataFrame using a mapper or by a Series of columns
Aggregate using one or more operations over the specified axis
Create a spreadsheet-style pivot table as a DataFrame
Resample time-series data and aggregate
Merge DataFrame or named Series objects with a database-style join

gotchas: df.groupby() on a categorical column includes all categories in the result even when some have zero rows, producing NaN aggregates that can silently break downstream mean calculations unless observed=True is passed.
df.resample() requires a DatetimeIndex; calling it on a DataFrame with a string or integer index raises a TypeError with a confusing message that does not mention setting the index first.
